title: Crumbly Resinous Cornish Chicken Pastry
description: A keto-friendly twist on the traditional Cornish pastry. This crumbly, resinous dish is perfect for a nostalgic British dinner.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine chicken breast, 1 tablespoon of bhuna curry paste, and 1 teaspoon of salt.
Mix well to combine and then set aside for 10 minutes.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of 3mm.
Cut out 4-6 squares of pastry, depending on the size you prefer for your pastries.
Place a tablespoon of chicken mixture onto one half of each pastry square, leaving a 1cm border around the edges.
Brush the edges with a little bit of water and then fold the other half of the pastry square over the filling, pressing the edges to seal.
Use a fork to crimp the edges and create a decorative border.
Place the pastries on a baking tray lined with parchment paper and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown.
Allow the pastries to cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve warm with a side salad or steamed vegetables.
This recipe makes 4-6 servings, depending on the size of your pastries.
Each serving contains approximately 450 calories, 35g of protein, 20g of carbs, and 30g of fat.
This recipe is easy to make and is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
It's also a great option for a special occasion, such as a Sunday roast or a holiday meal.
The combination of chicken, curry paste, and puff pastry creates a delicious and satisfying flavor profile that is sure to please even the pickiest eaters.
